title: microchilus quadratus
intro: Microchilus quadratus, commonly known as the Four-angled Orchid, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It is traditionally used in various folk medicine practices, particularly in Southeast Asia, due to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that M. quadratus possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as wound healing and the management of oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of M. quadratus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
parts: Microchilus quadratus has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been traditionally used in various cultures for their therapeutic properties. The roots of Microchilus quadratus are known to contain alkaloids, including aristolactam and aristolochic acid, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the flowers of Microchilus quadratus contain terpenoids and glycosides, which have been reported to exhibit antibacterial and anti-cancer activities.
side_effects: Microchilus quadratus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea and vomiting, due to its potential toxicity. Ingestion of the plant can also cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes and respiratory issues. Improper use of the plant may lead to gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. Additionally, excessive consumption of Microchilus quadratus can cause cardiac issues, including irregular heartbeat and decreased blood pressure.
